full_text:
EyeMT project updates

2.09.2025

Agenda

GeoMx updates
tCycIF troubleshooting
Next steps

GeoMx - updated preprocessing and normalisation

LOQ:
For b3 LOQ was much higher than in previous batches - possibly connected with impaired washing. Affects mostly samples collected in week 2. Exploration of ways to clean up the noisy signal without losing too many AOIs
Updated LOQ thresholds:
AOI has to contain at least 3% of detected genes (180 AOIs removed)
gene has to be present in 5% of AOIs
Final dataset (TLS excluded):
14174 genes 
1180 AOIs (out of 1433)
median gene nr is: 4842.5
mean gene nr is: 5058.45
median gene detection rate is: 0.259

GeoMx - updated preprocessing and normalisation

Normalisation:
Q3 normalisation (recommended for GeoMx, should lower the impact of noisy signal)
Deseq2 normalisation (golden standard for RNAseq)
Batch effect correction with harmony

GeoMx - updated preprocessing and normalisation

More stringent LOQ the seems to diminish patient-specific effect (slightly more for deseq2 normalisation)

GeoMx - scRNAseq reference dataset

Substantial number of fibroblasts are mistakenly labelled as tumor cells in Sciset reference scRNAseq dataset

Repeat downsampling
Use dataset from Hautaniemi lab (21 samples, 11 patients)

GeoMx - next steps

Clean up/use new reference for deconvolution
Finnish plugging in and run LR analysis
Re-label ROIs based on Cycif cell counts
Ladislav and GDR
Downstream analysis

GeoMx - preliminary results of pathway analysis

ssGSEA scores of selected HALLMARK (+ MHC) pathways vs fraction of cell types (full signal)
High correlation between numerous pathways and ct fractions (mostly CD8, Macrophages and DCs)
Many pathways correlated with >1 cell types fractions - will be explored further based on AOI labels
Many differences between pre vs post stroma and tumor

AOI labelling approach:
category with all AOIs containing ct of top 0.25 quartile 
Categories does not overlap!
Categories: 
CD8_Macro_DCs
CD8_Macro
CD8_DCs
Macro_DCs

tCycIF - TRIBUS VS MANUAL GATING

UMAPs on filtered and normalized channel data

Gating

TRIBUS

Speaker notes:
Check if filtering effects Tribus
Global level - all filtered cell types

UMAPs on filtered and normalized channel data

Next steps

Verification with Arttu’s GRUND: Effect of Incorrect Parameter Settings on Mesmer Segmentation
 Preliminary Findings:
Execution time is shorter when the parameter is set to correct image resolution.

Features to consider in downstream analysis

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11cuuYsxZUNHNDLnv4U6tTnHJ3ZTH6axG7OxfUosDgFg/edit?gid=1448520274#gid=1448520274
